description: PAGE TWELVE HISTORY OF EVENTS LEADING TO THE GREAT REVOLUTION Suspension of the Duma Brought Russian Revolt Long Struggle for Power of Liberal or Democratic Political Element Against ProPrussian Bureaucracy Pre ceded Overthrow of Government Unrest in Russia and disputes between the government and the Duma have oc curred repeatedly since the beginning of the European war and apparently the dissensions came to a head when Em peror Nicholas suspended the Duma and the Council of the Empire on March 12 The many changes in governmental places and the continual growth of lib eral feelings both in the Duma and Coun cil of the Empire led to predictions that a change in the fundamental government of the Russian Empire would come with the end of the war Affairs in Russia which had led to the installation of two premiers before March 19LG yhen Boris Stunner took the head of the government reached a crisis in November last year when Mr Stunner who was accused by liberal lead ers in Russfa of sympathies with Ger many was removed and his place taken by Mr Trepoff The removal of Mr Slurmer resulted from a clash between the Duma and the Cabinet in
